title: GET_CLIENTS
text: GET_CLIENTS Get static information about clients registered with the Uniface Router. operation GET_CLIENTS params string Using : IN ; (C1) string Host : IN ; (C256) string User : IN ; (C40) string UST : IN ; (C20) entity URCLIENTS.UROUTMON : OUT endparams Parameters Using —code indicating how the clients are identified; one of: C for clients with the specified host name, UST, and/or user name S for clients using servers with the specified host name, UST, and/or user name Host —host name of the client or server User —user name of the client or server UST —Uniface Server Type (UST) of the client or server URCLIENTS.UROUTMON—multiple occurrences of entity URCLIENTS.UROUTMON, containing client information. Table 1. URCLIENTS.UROUTMON Entity Fieldname Packing Code Data Type Description host C256 String Host name / IP address: primary key field 1 pid I4 Numeric Process ID: primary key field 2 ust C20 String Uniface server type (UST), (MON { PRIV } ) =Monitor, (MY MON { PRIV } ) =this Monitor, PRIV =privileged Monitor, (WEB Client) =WRD user C40 String O/S user name connet C3 String Connection network to Uniface Router (e.g. TCP ) conaddr C8 String Connection address on Uniface Router (e.g. the port number) server C80 String Server or hop, for example: ora:|u|p /ex /su or tcp:h|u|p|myust constamp E5 Datetime Client connected at dd-mmm-yyyy hh:mm:ss The order of clients is undefined. The selection criteria Host , User and/or UST , if present, are combined (logical AND) and applied to running servers or clients to select the list of clients. Return Values If successful, $status is set to the number (>=0) of returned URCLIENTS occurrences. Otherwise, see Return Codes . Use Allowed in all Uniface component types. Description The GET_CLIENTS operation gets static information about clients registered with the Uniface Router. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
